title: maria alta
intro: Maria alta, also known as Schinus molle, is a flowering plant native to South America, particularly in the Andes Mountains region. The maria alta plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help to reduce fever, soothe skin irritations, and combat infections. The plant is rich in flavonoids, tannins, and essential oils, making it beneficial for cardiovascular health, respiratory issues, and digestive problems. Herbal preparations of maria alta include teas, infusions, and topical creams, which can be used to relieve symptoms of colds, flu, and other respiratory conditions.
